zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter是Richard Walter和Margaret的女兒，於1564年9月18日出生在英國素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德。她於1585年6月28日與John Barker在英國素福爾可納易藍德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1640年6月11日在她的家鄉享年75歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter是Richard Walter和Margaret的女儿，于1564年9月18日出生在英国素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德。她于1585年6月28日与John Barker在英国素福爾可納易藍德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1640年6月11日在她的家乡享年75岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Walthers
meanings: German: patronymic from the personal name Walther (see Walter ).

variants.name: McQuate
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uait ‘son of Uat’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Irish form McQuaid .

variants.name: Waltke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Jurgen, Wilhelmina.
German: from a pet form of any of the ancient Germanic personal names formed with Old High German waltan ‘to rule’ as the first element, as in Walter .

variants.name: McWaters
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaitéir, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of the personal name Walter , with excrescent English -s.

variants.name: Wahler
meanings: German:
from the personal name Wol(d)er, Low German form of Walter .
habitational name for someone from any of various places called Wahl, Wahle, or Wahlen.

variants.name: McWalter
meanings: Irish and Scottish: from Gaelic Mac Uaitéir ‘son of Walter ’.

variants.name: Walters
meanings: English: variant of Walter , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
German: patronymic from   Walter  .

variants.name: McQuaid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Colum, Nuala.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaid ‘son of Uad’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Scottish form McQuate .

variants.name: Walther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Erwin, Wolfgang, Otto, Gerhard, Gunter, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Bernd, Bernhard.
German, Dutch, Swedish, and Danish: variant of   Walter  .

variants.name: Lauter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Erwin, Kurt, Sigismund, Wolf.

German: topographic name and habitational name, from a common stream name meaning ‘clear’, from Middle High German hlutra, or from any of the places called Lauter or Lauta (Saxony). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and some other European countries.
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian Lavtar or its Croatized and rare Slovenian variant Lautar: from a dialect variant of the personal name Valter (see Walter ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter was born on 18 September 1564, in Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England as the daughter of Richard Walter and Margaret. She married John Barker on 28 June 1585, in Nayland, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 11 June 1640, in her hometown, at the age of 75, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter est née le 18 septembre 1564, à Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre, fille de Richard Walter et Margaret. Elle a épousé John Barker le 28 juin 1585, à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 11 juin 1640, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter wurde am 18. September 1564 in Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England, als Tochter von Richard Walter und Margaret geboren. Sie hat am 28. Juni 1585 in Nayland, Suffolk, England John Barker geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 11. Juni 1640 im Alter von 75 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter è nata il il 18 settembre 1564 nel Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England come figlia di Richard Walter e Margaret. Lei sposato John Barker il 28 giugno 1585, nel Nayland, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 11 giugno 1640, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolta a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Walterは1564年9月18日にイギリススフォルクストク ビー ナイランドでRichard WalterとMargaretの娘として生まれた。1585年6月28日にイギリススフォルクナイランドでJohn Barkerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1640年6月11日に故郷で75歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter(은)는 1564년 9월 18일 영국 수폴크 스토크 비 나이랜드에서 Richard Walter(와)과 Margaret의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1585년 6월 28일 영국 수폴크 나이랜드에서 John Barker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1640년 6월 11일 그녀의 고향에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.18 September 1564–11 June 1640: 18 de setembro de 1564–11 de junho de 1640
pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter nasceu em 18 de setembro de 1564, em Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra filha de Richard Walter e Margaret. Ela casou-se com John Barker em 28 de junho de 1585, em Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 11 de junho de 1640, em sua cidade natal, com 75 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter родилась 18 сентября 1564 г. в местности сток би неиланд, суффолк, Англия. Её родителей звали Richard Walter и Margaret. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Barker 28 июня 1585 г. в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 11 июня 1640 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия.

es.18 September 1564–11 June 1640: 18 de septiembre de 1564–11 de junio de 1640
es.lifeSummary: Margaret Walter nació el 18 de septiembre de 1564, en Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra como hija de Richard Walter y Margaret. Se casó con John Barker el 28 de junio de 1585, en Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 11 de junio de 1640, en su pueblo, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra.
